questions.question: Define a circle and explain its basic elements.
questions.answer: A circle is the collection of all points in a plane that are at a constant distance from a fixed point called the centre. The basic elements of a circle include centre, radius, diameter, chord and circumference.

questions.question: Explain the terms radius and diameter of a circle.
questions.answer: The radius of a circle is the distance from the centre to any point on the circle. The diameter is the longest chord of the circle and is equal to twice the radius.

questions.question: What is a chord? Explain with example.
questions.answer: A chord is a line segment joining any two points on a circle. The diameter is a special chord passing through the centre.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of tangent to a circle.
questions.answer: A tangent to a circle is a line that touches the circle at exactly one point called the point of contact.

questions.question: What is a secant? How is it different from a tangent?
questions.answer: A secant is a line that intersects a circle at two points, whereas a tangent touches the circle at exactly one point.

questions.question: Explain the point of contact of a tangent.
questions.answer: The point at which a tangent touches the circle is called the point of contact.

questions.question: Explain why a tangent touches the circle at only one point.
questions.answer: If a line touches the circle at more than one point, it becomes a secant.

questions.question: Define the normal to a circle.
questions.answer: The line containing the radius through the point of contact of the tangent is called the normal to the circle.

questions.question: Explain concentric circles with an example.
questions.answer: Circles having the same centre but different radii are called concentric circles.

questions.question: Explain non-intersecting line with respect to a circle.
questions.answer: A line that does not meet the circle at any point is called a non-intersecting line.

questions.question: How many tangents can be drawn at a point on a circle? Explain.
questions.answer: Only one tangent can be drawn at a point on a circle.

questions.question: Explain the length of a tangent.
questions.answer: The distance between the external point and the point of contact is called the length of the tangent.

questions.question: State one property of a tangent to a circle.
questions.answer: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact.

questions.question: Explain why radius is drawn to the point of contact.
questions.answer: The radius drawn to the point of contact is perpendicular to the tangent.

questions.question: Explain the boundary of a circle.
questions.answer: The curved line forming the outer edge of the circle is called its boundary.

questions.question: Explain the real-life example of tangent using a wheel.
questions.answer: The wheel touches the ground at exactly one point, forming a tangent.

questions.question: Explain why every diameter is a chord.
questions.answer: Because it joins two points on the circle.

questions.question: Explain why every chord is not a diameter.
questions.answer: Because not all chords pass through the centre.

questions.question: Explain the term point outside a circle.
questions.answer: A point lying outside the boundary of the circle is called an external point.

questions.question: Explain the term internal point of a circle.
questions.answer: A point lying inside the boundary of the circle is called an internal point.

questions.question: Explain why no tangent can be drawn from an internal point.
questions.answer: Every line through an internal point intersects the circle at two points.

questions.question: Explain why two tangents can be drawn from an external point.
questions.answer: Two lines from the external point can touch the circle at exactly one point each.

questions.question: Explain why only one tangent can be drawn at a point on a circle.
questions.answer: Only one perpendicular can be drawn to the radius at a given point.

questions.question: Explain why tangent is a limiting case of secant.
questions.answer: When the two points of intersection of a secant coincide, it becomes a tangent.

questions.question: Explain the importance of studying tangents to a circle.
questions.answer: Tangents help in understanding contact between circular objects and straight lines.
